steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses
steps.content: The sentence contains two independent clauses that must be properly connected: “the exhibition set out to provide an overview of photography by women in the 1920s through the 1950s” and “its efforts were largely successful”. The second clause follows a participial phrase: “given the collection's breadth of more than 120 photos”.

steps.title: Match the comma-conjunction rule
steps.content: When a coordinating conjunction like “and” joins two independent clauses, a comma must come before it. Choice D correctly uses “1950s, and”, following this rule. Choices A, B, and C all fail: A uses a comma alone (comma splice), B has no punctuation (run-on), and C places “and” without the required comma. That matches choice D — D is correct.
